title: Add Internet Identity (Google-style) Login Flow to Login Page

requirements.text: Update the LoginPage to make the existing 'Continue with Google' button (currently UI-only) functional by wiring it to the Internet Identity login flow already implemented via useInternetIdentity. The button must trigger the login process, display a loading state while authentication is in progress, and show a user-facing error message (toast or inline) if login fails.

quotes: Add a 'Sign in with Google' button on the login page.
Show proper error messages if login fails.

acceptanceCriteria: The 'Sign in with Google' / 'Continue with Google' button on the login page is clickable and triggers Internet Identity authentication.
A loading spinner or disabled state is shown on the button while authentication is in progress.
If authentication fails, a visible error message is displayed to the user.
The button is styled consistently with the existing glassmorphism dark theme (bg-white/10, backdrop-blur-md, border border-white/20, violet/pink accent colors).

requirements.text: After successful Internet Identity login, if no user profile exists for the authenticated principal, automatically create a new profile using any available identity information. Prevent duplicate profiles by checking for an existing record tied to the same principal before creating a new one. Then redirect the user to the dashboard.

quotes: Allow new users to automatically create an account using their Google profile (name, email, profile picture).
Prevent duplicate accounts using the same email.
Redirect users to the dashboard after successful login.

acceptanceCriteria: First-time users are prompted to enter their display name and email to complete profile creation after Internet Identity login.
If a profile already exists for the authenticated principal, no duplicate is created and the user is taken directly to the dashboard.
After profile creation or confirmation of an existing profile, the user is redirected to /dashboard.
The backend createUserProfile function (or equivalent) enforces uniqueness by principal ID.

requirements.text: Ensure the login page and its 'Sign in with Google' / Internet Identity button layout are fully responsive and render correctly on mobile (320px+), tablet, and desktop screen sizes. The button must be easy to tap on touch devices with adequate padding and font size.

quotes: Make the UI modern and responsive, and ensure the Google login works on mobile, tablet, and desktop devices.

acceptanceCriteria: Login page layout is usable and visually correct at 320px, 768px, and 1280px viewport widths.
The authentication button has a minimum tap target of 44x44px on mobile.
No horizontal overflow or broken layout occurs on small screens.

constraints: Do not use Google OAuth 2.0 directly — the platform supports only Internet Identity for authentication.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All backend logic must remain in the single Motoko actor at backend/main.mo.
No external auth providers (Auth0, Firebase, etc.) may be introduced.
nonGoals: Direct Google OAuth 2.0 / Google API integration (not supported on this platform).
Storing Google profile pictures as external URLs fetched from Google servers.
Changes to the Admin Panel, Call Screen, or Practice Modes pages.
